=== FILE: /app/papers/PMC10448185.md ===
# Deletion of the CYP2D6 gene as a likely explanation for the serious side effects of the antipsychotic drug pimozide: a case report

## Metadata
**Authors:** Fernando Facal, Begoña Portela, Almudena Gil-Rodríguez, Francisco Barros, Olalla Maroñas, Angel Carracedo
**Journal:** Frontiers in Pharmacology
**Date:** 2023 Aug 10
**DOI:** [10.3389/fphar.2023.1237446](https://doi.org/10.3389/fphar.2023.1237446)
**PMID:** 37637419
**PMCID:** PMC10448185
**URL:** https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10448185/
**PDF:** [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10448185/pdf/fphar-14-1237446.pdf](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10448185/pdf/fphar-14-1237446.pdf)

## Abstract

CYP2D6 analysis prior to the prescription of pimozide is required above a certain dose by the Food and Drug Administration in order to detect individuals with the poor metabolizer status. This precautionary measure aims to prevent the occurrence of serious adverse drug reactions. This study presents a case of a patient diagnosed with schizophrenia spectrum disorder. The patient suffered re-admission in the psychiatry ward because of severe secondary symptoms due to the antipsychotic drug pimozide, previously prescribed on a first admission. In order to assess the patient’s medication profile, real-time PCR was performed to analyze the main genes responsible for its metabolization, namely, CYP2D6 and CYP3A4. The pharmacogenetic study revealed that the patient is a poor metabolizer for CYP2D6, presenting deletion of both copies of the gene (diplotype *5/*5). Fortunately, the symptomatology disappeared after the withdrawal of the responsible drug. In conclusion, abiding by the pharmacogenetic clinical practice guidelines and the pharmacogenetic analysis of CYP2D6 when prescribing pimozide would have probably saved the patient from the consequences of severe side effects and the health system expenditure. The output was too large to display in full (617KB). Here is a summary of what was found, along with the preview content:

The directory `/app/papers/` contains multiple markdown files. The first file previewed is:

---

**File: `/app/papers/PMC10448185.md`**

**Title:** Deletion of the CYP2D6 gene as a likely explanation for the serious side effects of the antipsychotic drug pimozide: a case report

**Authors:** Fernando Facal, Begoña Portela, Almudena Gil-Rodríguez, Francisco Barros, Olalla Maroñas, Angel Carracedo

**Journal:** Frontiers in Pharmacology
**Date:** 2023 Aug 10
**DOI:** 10.3389/fphar.2023.1237446
**PMID:** 37637419
**PMCID:** PMC10448185

**Abstract Summary:** This paper presents a case report of a patient with schizophrenia spectrum disorder who experienced severe adverse drug reactions to the antipsychotic pimozide. Pharmacogenetic testing revealed the patient had a CYP2D6 *5/*5 diplotype (deletion of both gene copies), making them a poor metabolizer. The FDA requires CYP2D6 analysis before prescribing pimozide above certain doses. The paper argues that adherence to pharmacogenetic clinical guidelines would have prevented the severe side effects and unnecessary healthcare costs.
